steps: Run 'python harness.py --bootstrap' from project root
Verify init.sh is created and executable
Verify feature_list.json is created with valid JSON
Verify claude-progress.txt is created with init entry
Verify .agent/runtime/initializer.done marker is created

description: Harness continuous loop mode runs without crashing
steps: Run 'python harness.py --run-forever --cycles 2' (limited cycles for test)
Verify harness executes at least one coding cycle
Verify logs are written to .agent/logs/
Verify harness.log entries are created in .agent/runtime/

description: Feature list and progress files are valid and tracked by git
steps: Verify feature_list.json is valid JSON array
Verify claude-progress.txt exists and is readable
Run 'git status' to confirm files are tracked

description: ReAct main loop implementation - Think step
steps: Agent receives a task and produces a thought/reasoning before any action
Thought is logged or observable in output
Agent does not execute tools without reasoning first

description: ReAct main loop implementation - Act step
steps: Agent can invoke a tool after reasoning
Tool call includes proper parameters matching schema
Tool result is captured for observation

description: ReAct main loop implementation - Observe step
steps: Agent receives and processes tool execution results
Observation influences next thought/action
Errors in tool execution are visible to agent
Agent can run basic shell commands (ls, pwd, echo)
Command output is returned to agent
Command errors are captured and reported

description: File tool - Read and write files
steps: Agent can read file contents
Agent can write/create files
Agent can list directory contents
Agent can make HTTP GET requests
Response content is returned to agent
Network errors are handled gracefully
When a tool call fails, agent can retry with modified parameters
Maximum retry count is configurable
Eventual failure is logged with context
Errors are categorized (transient, permanent, permission, etc.)
Transient errors trigger automatic retry
Permanent errors are reported with actionable suggestions
A constitution.md file defines allowed/disallowed behaviors
Constitution is loaded at agent startup
Constitution rules are versioned in git
Operations like rm -rf, format, delete database are flagged as high-risk
High-risk operations require explicit confirmation or rejection
Risk level is logged for audit
Each session/cycle has a unique trace ID
All significant actions are logged with trace ID
Logs can be searched by trace ID for debugging
High-risk commands can be executed in isolated environment
Sandbox limits file system access
Sandbox limits network access
User can send text messages to agent
Agent responses are displayed in chat format
Message history is preserved within session
User can create new sessions
User can switch between sessions
Each session maintains independent context
Agent responses are displayed as they are generated
User sees progressive text appearance
Streaming can be interrupted by user
Tool calls are displayed with tool name and parameters
Tool execution status is shown (running/success/failed)
Tool results are displayed in readable format
Code blocks are syntax highlighted
Generated files can be previewed
Artifacts can be downloaded
When agent encounters unrecoverable error, state is saved
Agent can resume from saved state
Recovery attempts are logged
Tasks can be queued for later execution
Higher priority tasks execute first
Queue state persists across restarts
Coder agent specializes in code generation and modification
Coder agent receives tasks from orchestrator
Coder agent reports completion status
Tester agent specializes in running tests and verification
Tester agent can validate coder's output
Tester agent reports test results
Reviewer agent specializes in code review
Reviewer agent provides feedback on code quality
Reviewer can approve or request changes
System handles 10+ concurrent sessions without degradation
Response time remains under 5s for typical operations
Memory usage stays within bounds
System runs continuously for 72 hours without crash
Memory leaks are within acceptable limits
All cycles complete successfully or log appropriate errors
Token usage is logged per session/cycle
Cost metrics are available in dashboard/logs
High-cost operations are flagged
Core functionality has automated regression tests
Tests run on each commit
Test results are reported in CI
Deployment steps are documented
Health check endpoints are available
Alerts are configured for critical failures
Agent remembers context within current session
Conversation history is maintained
Context window is managed efficiently
Agent can recall information from previous sessions
Task outcomes are persisted and searchable
Memory is indexed for efficient retrieval
Agent can connect to MCP servers
MCP tools are available to agent
MCP resources can be accessed
